parsers.c(3) Library Functions Manual parsers.c(3)

NAME

parsers.c - Custom option parsers implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct setting_map_entry_t
Lookup table for setting string-to-enum mapping.

Functions


bool parse_color_setting (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_utf8_setting (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_color_mode (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_color_filter (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_render_mode (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_render_theme (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_palette_type (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_log_level (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_port_option (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
int parse_server_bind_address (const char *arg, void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg)
Parse server bind address positional argument. int parse_client_address (const char *arg, void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg)
Parse client address positional argument. int parse_mirror_media (const char *arg, void *config, char **remaining, int num_remaining, char **error_msg)
Parse mirror mode positional argument (file path or URL). bool parse_palette_chars (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_verbose_flag (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_timestamp (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
Custom parser for --seek flag. bool parse_volume (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_log_file (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)
bool parse_audio_source (const char *arg, void *dest, char **error_msg)

Detailed Description

Custom option parsers implementation.

Definition in file parsers.c.

Function Documentation

bool parse_audio_source (const char * arg, void * dest, char ** error_msg)

Definition at line 1148 of file parsers.c.

1148                                                                        {
1149   if (!arg || !dest) {
1150     if (error_msg) {
1151       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
1152     }
1153     return false;
1154   }
1155 
1156   audio_source_t *audio_source = (audio_source_t *)dest;
1157   char lower[32];
1158   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
1159 
1160   // Auto (smart selection based on media state)
1161   if (strcmp(lower, "auto") == 0) {
1162     *audio_source = AUDIO_SOURCE_AUTO;
1163     return true;
1164   }
1165 
1166   // Microphone only
1167   if (strcmp(lower, "mic") == 0) {
1168     *audio_source = AUDIO_SOURCE_MIC;
1169     return true;
1170   }
1171 
1172   // Media only
1173   if (strcmp(lower, "media") == 0) {
1174     *audio_source = AUDIO_SOURCE_MEDIA;
1175     return true;
1176   }
1177 
1178   // Both microphone and media
1179   if (strcmp(lower, "both") == 0) {
1180     *audio_source = AUDIO_SOURCE_BOTH;
1181     return true;
1182   }
1183 
1184   if (error_msg) {
1185     *error_msg = platform_strdup("Audio source must be 'auto', 'mic', 'media', or 'both'");
1186   }
1187   return false;
1188 }




References platform_strdup().






int
  parse_client_address (const char * arg, void * config, char ** remaining, int
  num_remaining, char ** error_msg)


Parse client address positional argument. Implements the client
    address parsing logic from client.c. Parses [address][:port] with complex
    IPv6 handling.


Definition at line 673 of file parsers.c.


673                                                                                                                {
674   (void)remaining;
675   (void)num_remaining;
676 
677   if (!arg || !config) {
678     if (error_msg) {
679       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or config");
680     }
681     return -1;
682   }
683 
684   log_debug("parse_client_address: Processing argument: '%s'", arg);
685 
686   // Access address and port fields from options_state struct
687   char *address = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, address);
688   int *port = (int *)((char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, port));
689 
690   // Check for WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://) FIRST before session string validation
691   // WebSocket URLs are passed through without validation or port extraction
692   if (strncmp(arg, "ws://", 5) == 0 || strncmp(arg, "wss://", 6) == 0) {
693     log_debug("Detected WebSocket URL: %s", arg);
694     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
695     // For wss://, default to no ACIP-level encryption (TLS handles it)
696     if (strncmp(arg, "wss://", 6) == 0) {
697       bool *no_encrypt = (bool *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, no_encrypt);
698       *no_encrypt = true;
699       log_debug("Auto-detected wss:// - setting no_encrypt=true (TLS handles encryption)");
700     }
701     // Don't set port - WebSocket transport handles URL parsing internally
702     return 1; // Consumed 1 argument
703   }
704 
705   // Check for TCP URL (tcp://) - passed through directly to FFmpeg
706   // TCP URLs are used for streaming media from custom TCP servers
707   if (strncmp(arg, "tcp://", 6) == 0) {
708     log_debug("Detected TCP URL: %s", arg);
709     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
710     // Don't set port - TCP transport handles URL parsing internally
711     return 1; // Consumed 1 argument
712   }
713 
714   // Check if this is a session string (format: adjective-noun-noun)
715   // Session strings have exactly 2 hyphens, only lowercase letters, length 5-47
716   bool is_session = is_session_string(arg);
717   log_debug("parse_client_address: is_session_string('%s') = %s", arg, is_session ? "true" : "false");
718 
719   if (is_session) {
720     // This is a session string, not a server address
721     char *session_string = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, session_string);
722     SAFE_SNPRINTF(session_string, SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s", arg);
723     log_debug("parse_client_address: Stored session string: %s", arg);
724     return 1; // Consumed 1 arg
725   }
726 
727   // Not a session string, parse as server address
728   log_debug("parse_client_address: Parsing as server address (not a session string)");
729 
730   // Check for port in address (format: address:port or [ipv6]:port)
731   const char *colon = strrchr(arg, ':');
732 
733   if (colon != NULL) {
734     // Check if this is IPv6 with port [::1]:port or plain hostname:port
735     if (arg[0] == '[') {
736       // IPv6 with brackets: [address]:port
737       const char *closing_bracket = strchr(arg, ']');
738       if (closing_bracket && closing_bracket < colon) {
739         // Extract address (remove brackets)
740         size_t addr_len = (size_t)(closing_bracket - arg - 1);
741         if (addr_len >= OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE) {
742           if (error_msg) {
743             *error_msg = platform_strdup("IPv6 address too long");
744           }
745           return -1;
746         }
747         SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%.*s", (int)addr_len, arg + 1);
748 
749         // Extract and validate port
750         const char *port_str = colon + 1;
751         char *endptr;
752         long port_num = strtol(port_str, &endptr, 10);
753         if (*endptr != '\0' || port_num < 1 || port_num > 65535) {
754           if (error_msg) {
755             char msg[256];
756             safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid port number '%s'. Must be 1-65535.", port_str);
757             *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
758           }
759           return -1;
760         }
761         *port = (int)port_num;
762       }
763     } else {
764       // Check if it's IPv6 without brackets (no port allowed)
765       // or hostname/IPv4:port
766       size_t colon_count = 0;
767       for (const char *p = arg; *p; p++) {
768         if (*p == ':')
769           colon_count++;
770       }
771 
772       if (colon_count == 1) {
773         // Likely hostname:port or IPv4:port
774         size_t addr_len = (size_t)(colon - arg);
775         if (addr_len >= OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE) {
776           if (error_msg) {
777             *error_msg = platform_strdup("Address too long");
778           }
779           return -1;
780         }
781         SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%.*s", (int)addr_len, arg);
782 
783         // Extract and validate port
784         const char *port_str = colon + 1;
785         char *endptr;
786         long port_num = strtol(port_str, &endptr, 10);
787         if (*endptr != '\0' || port_num < 1 || port_num > 65535) {
788           if (error_msg) {
789             char msg[256];
790             safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid port number '%s'. Must be 1-65535.", port_str);
791             *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
792           }
793           return -1;
794         }
795         *port = (int)port_num;
796       } else {
797         // Multiple colons - likely bare IPv6 address
798         SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
799       }
800     }
801   } else {
802     // No colon - just an address
803     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
804   }
805 
806   // Validate addresses using comprehensive IPv4/IPv6 detection
807   bool has_dot = strchr(address, '.') != NULL;
808   bool has_colon = strchr(address, ':') != NULL;
809   bool starts_with_digit = address[0] >= '0' && address[0] <= '9';
810 
811   // Potential IPv6 address (has colons) - validate as IPv6
812   if (has_colon) {
813     if (!is_valid_ipv6(address)) {
814       if (error_msg) {
815         char msg[512];
816         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
817                       "Invalid IPv6 address '%s'.\n"
818                       "IPv6 addresses must be valid hex notation with colons.\n"
819                       "Examples: ::1, 2001:db8::1, fe80::1\n"
820                       "Or use hostnames like example.com",
821                       address);
822         *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
823       }
824       return -1;
825     }
826   } else if (has_dot && starts_with_digit) {
827     // Potential IPv4 address (has dots and starts with digit) - validate strictly
828     if (!is_valid_ipv4(address)) {
829       if (error_msg) {
830         char msg[512];
831         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
832                       "Invalid IPv4 address '%s'.\n"
833                       "IPv4 addresses must have exactly 4 octets (0-255) separated by dots.\n"
834                       "Examples: 127.0.0.1, 192.168.1.1\n"
835                       "For hostnames, use letters: example.com, localhost",
836                       address);
837         *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
838       }
839       return -1;
840     }
841   }
842   // Otherwise treat as valid hostname (no validation needed)
843 
844   // Note: Port conflict checking would require additional state
845   // (checking if --port flag was used). For now, this is a simplified version.
846   // Full implementation would need to track whether port was set via flag.
847 
848   log_debug("parse_client_address: Set address='%s', port=%d", address[0] ? address : "(empty)", *port);
849 
850   return 1; // Consumed 1 arg
851 }






References is_session_string(), is_valid_ipv4(),
    is_valid_ipv6(), platform_strdup(), and
    safe_snprintf().

bool parse_color_filter (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 269 of file parsers.c.


269                                                                        {
270   if (!arg || !dest) {
271     if (error_msg) {
272       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
273     }
274     return false;
275   }
276 
277   color_filter_t *color_filter = (color_filter_t *)dest;
278   char lower[32];
279   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
280 
281   // Try to match against all known color filters
282   *color_filter = color_filter_from_cli_name(lower);
283   if (*color_filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE || strcmp(lower, "none") == 0) {
284     return true;
285   }
286 
287   // Invalid value
288   if (error_msg) {
289     char msg[256];
290     safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
291                   "Invalid color filter '%s'. Valid values: none, black, white, green, magenta, fuchsia, "
292                   "orange, teal, cyan, pink, red, yellow",
293                   arg);
294     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
295   }
296   return false;
297 }

bool parse_color_mode (const char * arg, void * dest, char
  ** error_msg)


Definition at line 210 of file parsers.c.


210                                                                      {
211   if (!arg || !dest) {
212     if (error_msg) {
213       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
214     }
215     return false;
216   }
217 
218   terminal_color_mode_t *color_mode = (terminal_color_mode_t *)dest;
219   char lower[32];
220   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
221 
222   // Auto-detect
223   if (strcmp(lower, "auto") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "a") == 0) {
224     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
225     return true;
226   }
227 
228   // Monochrome/None
229   if (strcmp(lower, "none") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "mono") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "monochrome") == 0 ||
230       strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
231     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_NONE;
232     return true;
233   }
234 
235   // 16-color
236   if (strcmp(lower, "16") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "16color") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "ansi") == 0 ||
237       strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
238     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_16;
239     return true;
240   }
241 
242   // 256-color
243   if (strcmp(lower, "256") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "256color") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
244     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_256;
245     return true;
246   }
247 
248   // Truecolor
249   if (strcmp(lower, "truecolor") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "true") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "tc") == 0 ||
250       strcmp(lower, "rgb") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "24bit") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "3") == 0) {
251     *color_mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR;
252     return true;
253   }
254 
255   // Invalid value - suggest closest match
256   if (error_msg) {
257     char msg[256];
258     const char *suggestion = asciichat_suggest_enum_value("color-mode", arg);
259     if (suggestion) {
260       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid color mode '%s'. Did you mean '%s'?", arg, suggestion);
261     } else {
262       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid color mode '%s'. Valid values: auto, none, 16, 256, truecolor", arg);
263     }
264     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
265   }
266   return false;
267 }






References asciichat_suggest_enum_value(),
    platform_strdup(), and safe_snprintf().






bool parse_color_setting (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 160 of file parsers.c.


160                                                                         {
161   if (!dest) {
162     if (error_msg) {
163       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL destination");
164     }
165     return false;
166   }
167 
168   // Use generic parser with color setting lookup table
169   // Default to TRUE if no arg provided
170   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
171     int *color_setting = (int *)dest;
172     *color_setting = COLOR_SETTING_TRUE;
173     return true;
174   }
175 
176   return parse_setting_generic(arg, dest, g_color_setting_map, error_msg);
177 }

bool parse_log_file (const char * arg, void * dest, char
  ** error_msg)


Definition at line 1114 of file parsers.c.


1114                                                                    {
1115   if (!arg || !dest) {
1116     if (error_msg) {
1117       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
1118     }
1119     return false;
1120   }
1121 
1122   // Validate and normalize the log file path
1123   char *normalized = NULL;
1124   asciichat_error_t result = path_validate_user_path(arg, PATH_ROLE_LOG_FILE, &normalized);
1125 
1126   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1127     if (error_msg) {
1128       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1129       if (HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx)) {
1130         *error_msg = platform_strdup(err_ctx.context_message);
1131       } else {
1132         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Log file path validation failed");
1133       }
1134     }
1135     return false;
1136   }
1137 
1138   // Copy validated path to destination
1139   char *log_file_buf = (char *)dest;
1140   const size_t max_size = 256;
1141   SAFE_STRNCPY(log_file_buf, normalized, max_size - 1);
1142   log_file_buf[max_size - 1] = '\0';
1143 
1144   SAFE_FREE(normalized);
1145   return true;
1146 }

bool parse_log_level (const char * arg, void * dest, char
  ** error_msg)


Definition at line 446 of file parsers.c.


446                                                                     {
447   if (!dest) {
448     if (error_msg) {
449       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL destination");
450     }
451     return false;
452   }
453 
454   log_level_t *log_level = (log_level_t *)dest;
455 
456   // If no argument provided, use the default log level (based on build type)
457   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
458     *log_level = DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL;
459     return true;
460   }
461 
462   char lower[32];
463   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
464 
465   // Development level
466   if (strcmp(lower, "dev") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "development") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
467     *log_level = LOG_DEV;
468     return true;
469   }
470 
471   // Debug level
472   if (strcmp(lower, "debug") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "dbg") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
473     *log_level = LOG_DEBUG;
474     return true;
475   }
476 
477   // Info level
478   if (strcmp(lower, "info") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "information") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
479     *log_level = LOG_INFO;
480     return true;
481   }
482 
483   // Warning level
484   if (strcmp(lower, "warn") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "warning") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "3") == 0) {
485     *log_level = LOG_WARN;
486     return true;
487   }
488 
489   // Error level
490   if (strcmp(lower, "error") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "err") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "4") == 0) {
491     *log_level = LOG_ERROR;
492     return true;
493   }
494 
495   // Fatal level
496   if (strcmp(lower, "fatal") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "5") == 0) {
497     *log_level = LOG_FATAL;
498     return true;
499   }
500 
501   // Invalid value
502   if (error_msg) {
503     char msg[256];
504     safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid log level '%s'. Valid values: dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal", arg);
505     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
506   }
507   return false;
508 }






References platform_strdup(), and
  safe_snprintf().


Referenced by options_init().






int
  parse_mirror_media (const char * arg, void * config, char ** remaining, int
  num_remaining, char ** error_msg)


Parse mirror mode positional argument (file path or URL).
  Logic:



  
1.

  
Enforce mutual exclusion with --file and --url flags

  
2.

  
Check if argument is an accessible file path first

  
3.

  
If not a file, treat as URL (yt-dlp/ffmpeg will handle errors)

  
4.

  
Set media_file or media_url accordingly




Definition at line 866 of file parsers.c.


866                                                                                                              {
867   (void)remaining;
868   (void)num_remaining;
869 
870   if (!arg || !config) {
871     if (error_msg) {
872       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or config");
873     }
874     return -1;
875   }
876 
877   log_debug("parse_mirror_media: Processing argument: '%s'", arg);
878 
879   // Access media_file and media_url fields from options_state struct (same as other positional parsers)
880   char *media_file = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, media_file);
881   char *media_url = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, media_url);
882 
883   // Enforce mutual exclusion with --file flag
884   if (media_file[0] != '\0') {
885     if (error_msg) {
886       *error_msg = platform_strdup("cannot use both --file and a positional argument");
887     }
888     return -1;
889   }
890 
891   // Enforce mutual exclusion with --url flag
892   if (media_url[0] != '\0') {
893     if (error_msg) {
894       *error_msg = platform_strdup("cannot use both --url and a positional argument");
895     }
896     return -1;
897   }
898 
899   // Check if it's an accessible file path (priority 1)
900   if (platform_access(arg, PLATFORM_ACCESS_READ) == 0) {
901     log_debug("Argument '%s' is an accessible file", arg);
902     SAFE_SNPRINTF(media_file, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
903     return 1; // Consumed 1 argument
904   }
905 
906   // Not a file; treat as URL (yt-dlp/ffmpeg will handle any errors)
907   log_debug("Argument '%s' not found as file; treating as URL", arg);
908   SAFE_SNPRINTF(media_url, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", arg);
909   return 1; // Consumed 1 argument
910 }






References platform_access(), and
  platform_strdup().


Referenced by options_preset_unified().






bool parse_palette_chars (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 916 of file parsers.c.


916                                                                         {
917   if (!arg || !dest) {
918     if (error_msg) {
919       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
920     }
921     return false;
922   }
923 
924   // The dest pointer points to the palette_custom field in options_t
925   // We need to get the full options_t struct to call parse_palette_chars_option
926   // Since we only have the field pointer, we need to handle this directly
927 
928   char *palette_custom = (char *)dest;
929 
930   size_t len = strlen(arg);
931   if (len == 0) {
932     if (error_msg) {
933       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid palette-chars: value cannot be empty");
934     }
935     return false;
936   }
937 
938   if (len >= 256) {
939     if (error_msg) {
940       char msg[256];
941       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid palette-chars: too long (%zu chars, max 255)", len);
942       *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
943     }
944     return false;
945   }
946 
947   // Copy the palette characters
948   SAFE_STRNCPY(palette_custom, arg, 256);
949   palette_custom[255] = '\0';
950 
951   // Also set the palette type to custom by calculating back to options_t pointer
952   // dest points to options_t.palette_custom, so we can get options_t* using offset arithmetic
953   options_t *opts = (options_t *)((char *)dest - offsetof(options_t, palette_custom));
954   opts->palette_type = PALETTE_CUSTOM;
955 
956   return true;
957 }






References platform_strdup(), and
  safe_snprintf().






bool parse_palette_type (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 383 of file parsers.c.


383                                                                        {
384   if (!arg || !dest) {
385     if (error_msg) {
386       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
387     }
388     return false;
389   }
390 
391   palette_type_t *palette_type = (palette_type_t *)dest;
392   char lower[32];
393   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
394 
395   // Standard palette
396   if (strcmp(lower, "standard") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "std") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
397     *palette_type = PALETTE_STANDARD;
398     return true;
399   }
400 
401   // Blocks palette
402   if (strcmp(lower, "blocks") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "block") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
403     *palette_type = PALETTE_BLOCKS;
404     return true;
405   }
406 
407   // Digital palette
408   if (strcmp(lower, "digital") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "dig") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
409     *palette_type = PALETTE_DIGITAL;
410     return true;
411   }
412 
413   // Minimal palette
414   if (strcmp(lower, "minimal") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "min") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "3") == 0) {
415     *palette_type = PALETTE_MINIMAL;
416     return true;
417   }
418 
419   // Cool palette
420   if (strcmp(lower, "cool") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "4") == 0) {
421     *palette_type = PALETTE_COOL;
422     return true;
423   }
424 
425   // Custom palette
426   if (strcmp(lower, "custom") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "5") == 0) {
427     *palette_type = PALETTE_CUSTOM;
428     return true;
429   }
430 
431   // Invalid value - suggest closest match
432   if (error_msg) {
433     char msg[256];
434     const char *suggestion = asciichat_suggest_enum_value("palette", arg);
435     if (suggestion) {
436       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid palette type '%s'. Did you mean '%s'?", arg, suggestion);
437     } else {
438       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
439                     "Invalid palette type '%s'. Valid values: standard, blocks, digital, minimal, cool, custom", arg);
440     }
441     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
442   }
443   return false;
444 }






References asciichat_suggest_enum_value(),
    platform_strdup(), and safe_snprintf().


Referenced by set_palette().






bool parse_port_option (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 510 of file parsers.c.


510                                                                       {
511   if (!arg || !dest) {
512     if (error_msg) {
513       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
514     }
515     return false;
516   }
517 
518   int *port_value = (int *)dest;
519   uint16_t port_num;
520 
521   // Use the existing parse_port function for validation
522   asciichat_error_t err = parse_port(arg, &port_num);
523   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
524     if (error_msg) {
525       char msg[256];
526       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid port '%s'. Port must be a number between 0 and 65535 (0 = auto-assign).",
527                     arg);
528       *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
529     }
530     return false;
531   }
532 
533   *port_value = (int)port_num;
534   return true;
535 }






References parse_port(), platform_strdup(), and
    safe_snprintf().






bool parse_render_mode (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 299 of file parsers.c.


299                                                                       {
300   if (!arg || !dest) {
301     if (error_msg) {
302       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
303     }
304     return false;
305   }
306 
307   render_mode_t *render_mode = (render_mode_t *)dest;
308   char lower[32];
309   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
310 
311   // Foreground mode
312   if (strcmp(lower, "foreground") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "fg") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
313     *render_mode = RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND;
314     return true;
315   }
316 
317   // Background mode
318   if (strcmp(lower, "background") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "bg") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
319     *render_mode = RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND;
320     return true;
321   }
322 
323   // Half-block mode
324   if (strcmp(lower, "half-block") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "hb") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
325     *render_mode = RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK;
326     return true;
327   }
328 
329   // Invalid value - suggest closest match
330   if (error_msg) {
331     char msg[256];
332     const char *suggestion = asciichat_suggest_enum_value("render-mode", arg);
333     if (suggestion) {
334       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid render mode '%s'. Did you mean '%s'?", arg, suggestion);
335     } else {
336       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid render mode '%s'. Valid values: foreground, background, half-block",
337                     arg);
338     }
339     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
340   }
341   return false;
342 }






References asciichat_suggest_enum_value(),
    platform_strdup(), and safe_snprintf().






bool parse_render_theme (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 344 of file parsers.c.


344                                                                        {
345   if (!arg || !dest) {
346     if (error_msg) {
347       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
348     }
349     return false;
350   }
351 
352   int *theme = (int *)dest;
353   char lower[32];
354   to_lower(arg, lower, sizeof(lower));
355 
356   // Dark theme
357   if (strcmp(lower, "dark") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "0") == 0) {
358     *theme = 0; // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK
359     return true;
360   }
361 
362   // Light theme
363   if (strcmp(lower, "light") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "1") == 0) {
364     *theme = 1; // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT
365     return true;
366   }
367 
368   // Auto theme
369   if (strcmp(lower, "auto") == 0 || strcmp(lower, "2") == 0) {
370     *theme = 2; // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO
371     return true;
372   }
373 
374   // Invalid value
375   if (error_msg) {
376     char msg[256];
377     safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "Invalid render theme '%s'. Valid values: dark, light, auto", arg);
378     *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
379   }
380   return false;
381 }






References platform_strdup(), and
  safe_snprintf().






int
  parse_server_bind_address (const char * arg, void * config, char ** remaining,
  int num_remaining, char ** error_msg)


Parse server bind address positional argument. Implements the
    server bind address parsing logic from server.c. Can consume 1 argument per
    call, handling IPv4 or IPv6 bind addresses. The positional arg system will
    call this multiple times for multiple args.


Definition at line 551 of file parsers.c.


551                                                                                                                     {
552   if (!arg || !config) {
553     if (error_msg) {
554       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or config");
555     }
556     return -1;
557   }
558 
559   // Assume config struct has address and address6 fields (OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE each)
560   // This is a simplified version that assumes standard options_state layout
561   char *address = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, address);
562   char *address6 = (char *)config + offsetof(struct options_state, address6);
563 
564   int consumed = 0;
565 
566   // Parse first argument (IPv4 or IPv6)
567   char parsed_addr[OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE];
568   const char *addr_to_check = arg;
569   if (parse_ipv6_address(arg, parsed_addr, sizeof(parsed_addr)) == 0) {
570     addr_to_check = parsed_addr;
571   }
572 
573   // Check if it's IPv4 or IPv6
574   if (is_valid_ipv4(addr_to_check)) {
575     // Check if we already have a non-default IPv4 address
576     // Allow overwriting defaults (localhost, 0.0.0.0)
577     if (address[0] != '\0' && !is_localhost_ipv4(address) && strcmp(address, "localhost") != 0 &&
578         strcmp(address, "0.0.0.0") != 0) {
579       if (error_msg) {
580         char msg[256];
581         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
582                       "Cannot specify multiple IPv4 addresses.\n"
583                       "Already have: %s\n"
584                       "Cannot add: %s",
585                       address, addr_to_check);
586         *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
587       }
588       return -1;
589     }
590     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", addr_to_check);
591     consumed = 1;
592   } else if (is_valid_ipv6(addr_to_check)) {
593     // Check if we already have a non-default IPv6 address
594     // Allow overwriting default (::1)
595     if (address6[0] != '\0' && !is_localhost_ipv6(address6)) {
596       if (error_msg) {
597         char msg[256];
598         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
599                       "Cannot specify multiple IPv6 addresses.\n"
600                       "Already have: %s\n"
601                       "Cannot add: %s",
602                       address6, addr_to_check);
603         *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
604       }
605       return -1;
606     }
607     SAFE_SNPRINTF(address6, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", addr_to_check);
608     consumed = 1;
609   } else {
610     if (error_msg) {
611       char msg[512];
612       safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg),
613                     "Invalid IP address '%s'.\n"
614                     "Server bind addresses must be valid IPv4 or IPv6 addresses.\n"
615                     "Examples:\n"
616                     "  ascii-chat server 0.0.0.0\n"
617                     "  ascii-chat server ::1\n"
618                     "  ascii-chat server 0.0.0.0 ::1",
619                     arg);
620       *error_msg = platform_strdup(msg);
621     }
622     return -1;
623   }
624 
625   // Try to parse second address if available
626   if (remaining && num_remaining > 0 && remaining[0]) {
627     const char *second_arg = remaining[0];
628     memset(parsed_addr, 0, sizeof(parsed_addr));
629     addr_to_check = second_arg;
630     if (parse_ipv6_address(second_arg, parsed_addr, sizeof(parsed_addr)) == 0) {
631       addr_to_check = parsed_addr;
632     }
633 
634     if (is_valid_ipv4(addr_to_check)) {
635       // Second is IPv4
636       if (address[0] != '\0' && !is_localhost_ipv4(address) && strcmp(address, "localhost") != 0 &&
637           strcmp(address, "0.0.0.0") != 0) {
638         // Already have an IPv4, can't add another
639         return consumed;
640       }
641       if (is_valid_ipv4(arg)) {
642         // First was also IPv4, can't have two IPv4s
643         return consumed;
644       }
645       // First was IPv6, second is IPv4 - accept both
646       SAFE_SNPRINTF(address, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", addr_to_check);
647       consumed = 2;
648     } else if (is_valid_ipv6(addr_to_check)) {
649       // Second is IPv6
650       if (address6[0] != '\0' && !is_localhost_ipv6(address6)) {
651         // Already have an IPv6, can't add another
652         return consumed;
653       }
654       if (is_valid_ipv6(arg)) {
655         // First was also IPv6, can't have two IPv6s
656         return consumed;
657       }
658       // First was IPv4, second is IPv6 - accept both
659       SAFE_SNPRINTF(address6, OPTIONS_BUFF_SIZE, "%s", addr_to_check);
660       consumed = 2;
661     }
662   }
663 
664   return consumed;
665 }






References is_localhost_ipv4(), is_localhost_ipv6(),
    is_valid_ipv4(), is_valid_ipv6(), parse_ipv6_address(),
    platform_strdup(), and safe_snprintf().


Referenced by options_preset_unified().






bool parse_timestamp (const char * arg, void * dest, char
  ** error_msg)


Custom parser for --seek flag. Accepts both
    "hh:mm:ss.ms" format and plain seconds format. Examples:


    
  
  • "30" = 30 seconds
    • 
  
  • "30.5" = 30.5 seconds
    • 
  
  • "1:30" = 1 minute 30 seconds (90 seconds)
    • 
  
  • "1:30.5" = 1 minute 30.5 seconds (90.5 seconds)
    • 
  
  • "0:1:30.5" = 1 minute 30.5 seconds (90.5 seconds)
    • 
  
  • "1:2:30.5" = 1 hour 2 minutes 30.5 seconds (3750.5 seconds)
    • 



Definition at line 997 of file parsers.c.


997                                                                     {
998   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
999     if (error_msg) {
1000       *error_msg = platform_strdup("--seek requires a timestamp argument");
1001     }
1002     return false;
1003   }
1004 
1005   double *timestamp = (double *)dest;
1006   char *endptr;
1007   long strtol_result;
1008 
1009   // Count colons to determine format
1010   int colon_count = 0;
1011   for (const char *p = arg; *p; p++) {
1012     if (*p == ':')
1013       colon_count++;
1014   }
1015 
1016   if (colon_count == 0) {
1017     // Plain seconds format: "30" or "30.5"
1018     *timestamp = strtod(arg, &endptr);
1019     if (*endptr != '\0' || *timestamp < 0.0) {
1020       if (error_msg) {
1021         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected non-negative seconds");
1022       }
1023       return false;
1024     }
1025     return true;
1026   } else if (colon_count == 1) {
1027     // MM:SS or MM:SS.ms format
1028     strtol_result = strtol(arg, &endptr, 10);
1029     if (*endptr != ':' || strtol_result < 0) {
1030       if (error_msg) {
1031         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected MM:SS or MM:SS.ms format");
1032       }
1033       return false;
1034     }
1035     long minutes = strtol_result;
1036     double seconds = strtod(endptr + 1, &endptr);
1037     if (*endptr != '\0' && *endptr != '.' && *endptr != '\0') {
1038       if (error_msg) {
1039         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected MM:SS or MM:SS.ms format");
1040       }
1041       return false;
1042     }
1043     *timestamp = minutes * 60.0 + seconds;
1044     return true;
1045   } else if (colon_count == 2) {
1046     // HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.ms format
1047     strtol_result = strtol(arg, &endptr, 10);
1048     if (*endptr != ':' || strtol_result < 0) {
1049       if (error_msg) {
1050         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.ms format");
1051       }
1052       return false;
1053     }
1054     long hours = strtol_result;
1055 
1056     strtol_result = strtol(endptr + 1, &endptr, 10);
1057     if (*endptr != ':' || strtol_result < 0 || strtol_result >= 60) {
1058       if (error_msg) {
1059         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: minutes must be 0-59");
1060       }
1061       return false;
1062     }
1063     long minutes = strtol_result;
1064 
1065     double seconds = strtod(endptr + 1, &endptr);
1066     if (*endptr != '\0') {
1067       if (error_msg) {
1068         *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp: expected HH:MM:SS or HH:MM:SS.ms format");
1069       }
1070       return false;
1071     }
1072     *timestamp = hours * (double)SEC_PER_HOUR + minutes * (double)SEC_PER_MIN + seconds;
1073     return true;
1074   } else {
1075     if (error_msg) {
1076       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid timestamp format: too many colons");
1077     }
1078     return false;
1079   }
1080 }






References platform_strdup().






bool parse_utf8_setting (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 191 of file parsers.c.


191                                                                        {
192   if (!dest) {
193     if (error_msg) {
194       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL destination");
195     }
196     return false;
197   }
198 
199   // Use generic parser with UTF-8 setting lookup table
200   // Default to TRUE if no arg provided
201   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
202     int *utf8_setting = (int *)dest;
203     *utf8_setting = UTF8_SETTING_TRUE;
204     return true;
205   }
206 
207   return parse_setting_generic(arg, dest, g_utf8_setting_map, error_msg);
208 }






References platform_strdup().






bool parse_verbose_flag (const char * arg, void * dest,
  char ** error_msg)


Definition at line 959 of file parsers.c.


959                                                                        {
960   (void)error_msg; // Unused but required by function signature
961 
962   // If arg is NULL or starts with a flag, just increment
963   // Otherwise try to parse as integer count
964   unsigned short int *verbose_level = (unsigned short int *)dest;
965 
966   if (!arg || arg[0] == '\0') {
967     // No argument provided, just increment
968     (*verbose_level)++;
969     return true;
970   }
971 
972   // Try to parse as integer count
973   char *endptr;
974   long value = strtol(arg, &endptr, 10);
975   if (*endptr == '\0' && value >= 0 && value <= 100) {
976     *verbose_level = (unsigned short int)value;
977     return true;
978   }
979 
980   // If it didn't parse as int, treat as flag increment
981   (*verbose_level)++;
982   return true;
983 }










bool parse_volume (const char * arg, void * dest, char **
  error_msg)


Definition at line 1082 of file parsers.c.


1082                                                                  {
1083   if (!arg || !dest) {
1084     if (error_msg) {
1085       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Internal error: NULL argument or destination");
1086     }
1087     return false;
1088   }
1089 
1090   float *volume = (float *)dest;
1091   char *endptr;
1092   float val = strtof(arg, &endptr);
1093 
1094   if (*endptr != '\0' || arg == endptr) {
1095     if (error_msg) {
1096       *error_msg = platform_strdup("Invalid volume value. Must be a number between 0.0 and 1.0");
1097     }
1098     return false;
1099   }
1100 
1101   if (val < 0.0f || val > 1.0f) {
1102     if (error_msg) {
1103       char buf[256];
1104       SAFE_SNPRINTF(buf, sizeof(buf), "Volume must be between 0.0 and 1.0 (got %.2f)", val);
1105       *error_msg = platform_strdup(buf);
1106     }
1107     return false;
1108   }
1109 
1110   *volume = val;
1111   return true;
1112 }






References platform_strdup().
